content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as both a linguistic reflection of thought and a metacognitive practice to refine writing, the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework can be updated to integrate LLM self-reflection mechanisms by drawing a parallel: a reflexion agent’s dynamic self-memory should act like a language learner’s reflective vocabulary notebook, capturing inconsistent solution perspectives from LLM self-reflection to iteratively refine future responses. This turns generic self-reflection into a structured, incremental skill-building practice analogous to second language vocabulary acquisition.
